channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Experts share strategies to protect your household budget during SA’s rising living costs
item.description: As South Africans face yet another hike in fuel prices, households are being urged to reassess their spending habits and prioritise long-term stability over short-term comfort. The article features in...

item.title: 12-Year-Old Hero Saves Classmates During Tumbler Ridge Shooting Tragedy
item.description: More than a month after the tragic mass shooting in Tumbler Ridge, stories of bravery are emerging, particularly one of a 12-year-old student named Christina. At the National Prayer Breakfast in Ottaw...

item.title: Tanzania researchers working on TB treatment that could take only three months
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Tanzania dey see hope say treatment for Tuberculosis (TB) fit soon reduce from the normal six months to as short as three months, thanks to ongoing clinical trials. Researchers at Ifak...

item.title: Gunman Behind Killing of Ex-Security Aide of Isko Moreno Arrested
item.description: Authorities have arrested Albert Magdaraog, the suspected gunman involved in the murder of George Capistrano, a former police officer, in Tondo, Manila. The arrest was confirmed by Manila Mayor Isko M...

item.title: India Reports Progress in Tuberculosis Control Through Mass Screening Initiatives
item.description: India continues to face a significant challenge from tuberculosis, which accounts for roughly 25% of the global TB burden, making it the country with the highest number of TB cases worldwide. Despite ...

item.title: Jets Defeat Golden Knights 4-1 to Keep Playoff Hopes Alive
item.description: The Winnipeg Jets secured a crucial 4-1 victory against the Vegas Golden Knights, despite not getting the help they needed from other teams in the playoff race. It was their second win in a row, with ...

item.title: India Has Adequate Fuel Reserves Amid Global Energy Disruptions, Says BPCL
item.description: Amid concerns over potential disruptions in global fuel supply due to the ongoing war between the US, Israel, and Iran, Indian oil companies have assured the public that there is no shortage of petrol...

item.title: Bacolod broadcaster Fred Salanga, known as ‘Aksyon Fred,’ passes away at 77
item.description: Fred Salanga, famously known as 'Aksyon Fred,' a veteran radio broadcaster in Bacolod and Negros Occidental, passed away on March 23, 2026, at age 77 due to lung cancer. His death comes shortly after ...

item.title: Alden Richards earns respect from Nico Bolzico and Wil Dasovich in triathlon relay
item.description: During the recent Ironman Davao triathlon relay, Filipino personalities Nico Bolzico and Wil Dasovich expressed admiration for Alden Richards' athletic performance, despite initial doubts about his ab...

item.title: Kyle and Kendall Lewis: Twin Doctors Who Studied and Work Together
item.description: Kyle and Kendall Lewis, identical twin brothers from Slidell, Louisiana, have achieved an impressive milestone by graduating together from medical school and now working at the same hospital. Their jo...

item.title: Jeremiah Kioni Praises Rigathi Gachagua’s Leadership Compared to Raila Odinga’s Opposition Years
item.description: Jubilee secretary general Jeremiah Kioni has publicly praised Rigathi Gachagua, claiming that the DCP leader has achieved more in his one year of political activism than Raila Odinga did during over 3...

item.title: Digital transformation in South Africa fails due to leadership, not technology
item.description: In South Africa, the persistent challenges of inequality, unemployment, and poverty are often misattributed to a lack of technology. Dr Stella Bvuma argues that the real obstacle to effective digital ...

item.title: Robinsons Retail to Close All No Brand Stores in the Philippines by June 2026
item.description: Robinsons Retail Holdings Inc. (RRHI), part of the Gokongwei Group and the Philippines’ second-largest retail conglomerate, announced the closure of all 11 standalone No Brand stores in the country by...

item.title: Guidance for Councils on Designing Safer Streets for Women and Girls
item.description: Councils in England are set to receive new guidance on redesigning streets to make them safer for women and girls. Active Travel England (ATE) is developing this guidance, which aims to tackle systemi...

item.title: Trump discusses possible Iran-U.S. negotiations as tensions persist over the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump revealed that Iran had offered what he described as a valuable 'present' connected to energy routes through the Strait of Hormuz, a move he presented as a sign of goodwill ...

item.title: Human Remains of Manitoba Soldier from WWI Identified After More Than 100 Years
item.description: The remains of Albert Henry Detmold, a Manitoba soldier killed during the First World War, have been identified after being uncovered during a construction project in Loos-en-Gohelle, France, in 2020....

item.title: India Faces Rising Obesity Crisis Despite Misconceptions as Lifestyle Issue
item.description: India is grappling with a serious obesity problem, where one in four Indians is overweight, yet many still view it merely as a lifestyle choice rather than a chronic disease. This was highlighted at a...

item.title: Mark Scheifele Guides Jets to 4-1 Victory Over Golden Knights
item.description: The Winnipeg Jets secured an impressive 4-1 victory over the Vegas Golden Knights, with forward Mark Scheifele playing a pivotal role in leading his team to the win. The match, held on March 24, 2026,...

item.title: Tanzania Plans Zero Waste Campaign for the Food Sector
item.description: Tanzania is preparing for the International Day of Zero Waste, with a special focus on reducing waste in the food sector. The National Environment Management Council (NEMC), in collaboration with the ...

item.title: Analysis: Trump’s 15-Point Iran Proposal Draws Doubts Amid Outdated Framework
item.description: Diplomats familiar with US-Iran negotiations believe that Donald Trump’s latest 15-point framework for peace with Iran largely repeats a plan originally proposed in May 2025. That plan, drafted by Tru...
